This phrase means right from the beginning or the very start of something.
It's often used to emphasize that something has been the case since the very beginning.
For example, I knew I would enjoy this project from the get-go.
Remember you can use, from the get-go, when you want to highlight the starting point or
the initial stage of an event or situation.

The next expression is, I'm all ears.
This phrase means that someone is eager and ready to listen or hear what you have to say.
It's often used to show that you are paying full attention and are interested in what
the other person has to communicate.
For example, tell me your idea, I'm all ears.
Remember you can use, I'm all ears, when you want to convey your openness and attentiveness
to someone's thoughts or suggestion.
